vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential stress concentration at wing spar socket.
risk.mit: Perform FEA to confirm stress distribution and add fillets if necessary.

risk.desc: GPS boom mount may interfere with landing gear deployment.
risk.mit: Review landing gear clearance and GPS boom placement.

risk.desc: Belly camera window may be prone to damage during ground operations.
risk.mit: Add protective cover or relocate camera window.

risk.desc: Avionics bay cooling may be insufficient in high ambient temperatures.
risk.mit: Increase airflow through avionics bay or add active cooling.

risk.desc: Fuselage skin thickness may not provide adequate impact resistance.
risk.mit: Conduct impact testing and adjust thickness if needed.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads from wing spar into fuselage spine
Cooling air from avionics bay to belly camera window
Dissipation of avionics heat through fuselage spine
V-tail motion from wing spar socket to v-tail control surfaces
GPS boom mounting and service access

integration_opportunities.description: Optimize load paths from wing spar to fuselage spine
Increase airflow through avionics bay and belly camera window
Evaluate CFRP vs. Al for weight reduction

verification_focus.description: Validate load paths from wing spar to fuselage spine
Simulate airflow through avionics bay and belly camera window
Perform stress analysis on fuselage spine and wing spar socket
Inspect bolted interfaces for proper clearance and preload
Ensure GPS boom and belly camera window are easily accessible

physics_paths.path_details: Loads from nacelle attach points route through wing spars into fuselage spine
Motor and servo service access through removable access covers
Heat from electronics and motor dissipated through wing structure to ambient air
Nacelle rotation clearance and bearing seats for smooth motion
Structural integrity to resist aerodynamic pressure

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate nacelle mounting points into wing spar structure
Integrate sensor avionics into sensor pockets for monitoring wing health
Ensure access for maintenance and service through access covers

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: CMM for critical dimensions, visual inspection for surface finish

verification_focus.description: Verify mass estimation against design requirements
